message:
fix(observability): exclude distance correlation from severity indicator count

Distance correlation was being counted as both an active_indicator and
a strong_indicator in the spectral health severity assessment, alongside
Hoeffding's D, Kendall's tau, and Spearman's rho. However, distance
correlation is highly correlated with these other rank-based measures —
they all detect time-value dependence — so counting it as a separate
indicator inflated severity scores with no genuinely new evidence.

A simple linear trend would push severity from Warning to Critical
solely because four highly-correlated metrics all fired instead of three,
which misrepresents the actual risk level.

The fix removes distance_corr from the active/strong indicator counting
while preserving its contribution to the confidence score, where it adds
unique information through raw metric distances rather than ranks. This
keeps the overall detection sensitivity intact while making severity
levels more accurately reflect the diversity of evidence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
